title: Learning support - Ministry of Education
content:
Te puna tautoko i te ako
Find out about the resources, tools and assistance available if your child needs support with their learning.
- Specialist services 4
- Learning and curriculum 2
- Deaf and hard of hearing 1
- Te whai āwhina ako hei te pikinga ki taumata kura kē atu Continuing with learning support when moving to the next school level We work with schools, kura, and whānau | families to make sure children and young people get the right support when they need it.
We work with schools, kura, and whānau | families to make sure children and young people get the right support when they need it.
- Ngā arawātea ako mā ngā tamariki me ngā kōhungahunga ihumanea Learning opportunities for gifted children and young people There are awards, and events and opportunities, for gifted ākonga | students to extend their learning outside normal schoolwork.
There are awards, and events and opportunities, for gifted ākonga | students to extend their learning outside normal schoolwork.
- Ngā taunakitanga ako mō te pānui me te whakawhitinga Learning support for literacy and communication All children are unique in how they learn literacy like reading and writing. Schools adapt the way they teach to meet strengths and needs.
All children are unique in how they learn literacy like reading and writing. Schools adapt the way they teach to meet strengths and needs.
- Te pūnaha pīra i te Kaupapa Tukuroa i ngā Rauemi Section 47 Reconsideration process for the Ongoing Resourcing Scheme Parents or caregivers can make a formal request to the Secretary of Education for a reconsideration of the decision under Section 47 of the Education and Training Act 2020 (The Act).
Parents or caregivers can make a formal request to the Secretary of Education for a reconsideration of the decision under Section 47 of the Education and Training Act 2020 (The Act).
- Ngā kura hāpai Specialist schools Specialist schools provide support to students with complex needs. They deliver support on campus, in partner schools, or in the home.
Specialist schools provide support to students with complex needs. They deliver support on campus, in partner schools, or in the home.
- Te hāpai ako mā ngā ākonga Turi, mā ngā ākonga āhua turi rānei Support with education for students who are Deaf or hard of hearing Children who are Deaf or hard of hearing can get support with their education until they leave school.
Children who are Deaf or hard of hearing can get support with their education until they leave school.
- Ngā mahi hei tautoko i ngā tamariki me ngā kōhungahunga ihumanea ki te kura Supporting gifted children and young people at school Gifted students have exceptional talents but can face challenges at school. Find out how you can support them to reach their full potential.
Gifted students have exceptional talents but can face challenges at school. Find out how you can support them to reach their full potential.
- Te tautoko i tō tamaiti mehemea me whai āwhina atu anō ia i roto i āna mahi ako Supporting your child if they need extra help with their learning Schools and kura have many resources and tools they can access to support learners, including our learning support team.
Schools and kura have many resources and tools they can access to support learners, including our learning support team.
- Te Kahu Tōī Ratonga Torowhārahi Whāomoomo Te Kahu Tōī Intensive Wraparound Service Te Kahu Tōī Intensive Wraparound Service (IWS) is for a small number of young people in Years 0 to 10 with highly complex needs.
Te Kahu Tōī Intensive Wraparound Service (IWS) is for a small number of young people in Years 0 to 10 with highly complex needs.
- Ngā tautoko ā-waka mā ngā tamariki whai matea haumaru Transport assistance for children with safety or mobility needs We can assist you if your child can't travel to school independently. Find out what we can do and how to apply for support.
We can assist you if your child can't travel to school independently. Find out what we can do and how to apply for support.
